title: Humata nephrodioides
taxonomy: Humata nephrodioides belongs to the family Arecaceae. It is a type of climbing palm native to Southeast Asia. This species is classified under the genus Humata. Further classification includes it being a member of the order Arecales and class Liliopsida. The kingdom to which it belongs is Plantae.

It's also thought to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a possible treatment for certain infections. More studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential effects of Humata nephrodioides.

intro: Humata nephrodioides, also known as Gotu Kola, is a traditional medicinal plant that belongs to the family of Polypodiaceae, a type of fern native to tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Gotu Kola include its potential to improve cognitive function, boost immune system, and promote wound healing, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoid saponins, particularly asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and brahmic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gotu Kola is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and topical creams, which are used to promote skin health, improve cognitive function, and support overall well-being.
